strait.name: Strait of Hormuz
strait.closure_type: De facto (insurance-driven + active attacks on vessels)
strait.closure_method: Asymmetric denial via drone and missile strikes on commercial vessels, triggering insurance-driven shutdown. IRGC used sea drones, anti-ship missiles, and shore-launched projectiles to strike 21+ vessels since Feb 28. War risk insurance withdrawn or repriced at ~5% of vessel value — a 100x increase from pre-war 0.02-0.05%. For a $100M tanker, $5M per transit. At least one tanker paid $2M for transit rights through Iran-approved Larak Island corridor. Trump ordered DFC to provide $20B in political risk reinsurance (Chubb as lead underwriter), but JPMorgan estimates actual exposure at $352B across 329 tankers. Reports of naval mine deployment began ~March 10; US destroyed 44+ Iranian minelayers. Iran's stockpile estimated at 2,000-6,000 naval mines. Iran retains 80%+ of its small boats and minelayers. London Joint War Committee extended high-risk area to include waters around Oman.
strait.status: Day 48: BLOCKADE DAY 4 — 13-14 VESSELS TURNED BACK IN 72 HOURS — USS SPRUANCE INTERDICTS IRANIAN-FLAGGED CARGO VESSEL / IRGC DECLARES 3-SEA COUNTER-BLOCKADE DOCTRINE / HEGSETH: LOCKED AND LOADED. CENTCOM blockade tally: 6 first 24 hrs → 9 in 48 hrs → 13-14 in 72 hrs. USS Spruance (DDG-111) turned back an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Gulf of Oman Apr 14 (Stars & Stripes, Apr 16) — 10th interception; 20+ non-Iran-bound commercial ships passed freely. BBC Verify/MarineTraffic disputes CENTCOM's 'zero penetrations' claim — at least 4 Iran-related ships transited the Strait Apr 14; Chinese-flagged Rich Starry U-turned at the Gulf of Oman; Elpis stopped near the Iranian coast; two tankers stopped at Chabahar; the Iraq-bound supertanker Agios Fanourios I became the first crude carrier to head west post-blockade (gCaptain). Hormuz transit volume below 10% of pre-war average. CENTCOM reports >10,000 personnel enforcing blockade with 'more than a dozen warships and dozens of aircraft.' USS Pioneer and USS Chief dispatched from Singapore for mine clearance. IRGC Maj. Gen. Ali Abdollahi (Khatam al-Anbiya HQ) declared Apr 15 on Iranian state TV a 'three-sea' counter-blockade doctrine: Persian Gulf + Sea of Oman + Red Sea — 'will not allow any exports or imports to continue' — described as 'prelude to violation of ceasefire' (Lloyd's List, Al Jazeera, NBC). Mojtaba Khamenei 'military adviser' warned Iran would 'sink American ships in the Strait of Hormuz.' Iran's National Petrochemical Co suspended all petrochemical exports 'until further notice' Apr 15. Iran's armed forces continue to describe the blockade as 'an illegal act amounting to piracy.' Trump Apr 15 called Iran's blockade response 'pretty amazing'; Trump (Fox Apr 15) claimed UK and 'a couple of other countries' are sending minesweepers — the UK government EXPLICITLY told BBC it will not assist the blockade. Macron/France and UK are instead building a separate ~15-country freedom-of-navigation coalition under UN framework. Australia declines. China MFA: 'dangerous and irresponsible'; Lavrov (Beijing): 'no military solution.' Treasury Apr 15: secondary sanctions — 3 individuals, 17 entities, 9 oil tankers tied to Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani; 30-day waivers on Iranian/Russian oil halted. Hegseth (Pentagon Apr 16): US 'locked and loaded' to restart combat if talks collapse; WSJ reports Trump weighing 'resumption of limited military strikes' — NOT implemented. No mine incidents, no missile launches at US vessels, no Iranian tanker seizures Apr 15-16. IEA April OMR: 10.1 mb/d March supply loss — largest disruption in history; Hormuz throughput 3.8 mb/d (80%+ decline); OPEC+ output fell 7.9 mb/d in March. Physical dated Brent traded $131.97/bbl against ~$96 futures = $35 physical premium = scarcity under blockade. EIA April STEO: Brent peaks at $115/bbl Q2 2026. Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~216 hours (expires ~Apr 21-22).

impact.tankers_transiting_current_note: ~165 total vessels since Mar 1 vs normal ~5,000+. Hormuz RE-CLOSED by IRGC Apr 8 after Israel's 'Operation Eternal Darkness' in Lebanon. Apr 7: 11 vessels transited (4 sanctioned Iranian-trading inbound; 7 outbound including 3 tankers, 4 bulk carriers) via IRGC-controlled Northern Corridor near Larak. Apr 8: only 5 bulk carriers outbound by noon — NO major oil tankers from blue-chip operators. MarineTraffic identified just 2 by name (NJ Earth Greek-owned, Daytona Beach Liberia-flagged). ~800 tankers and 3,200 total vessels with ~20,000 seafarers stuck in Persian Gulf (Bloomberg Apr 8). War-risk insurance unavailable; effectively blocks commercial operators regardless of Iran's position. Shipping execs to CNBC: 'We have no information about how we could transit.' Iran and Oman plan transit fees/tolls for reconstruction. Windward: 'supervised pause, not reopened.'
impact.insurance_cost_note: War-risk premiums surged 300%+, with coverage for $100M tanker jumping from ~$200,000 to $1-5M per voyage. Major P&I clubs (Gard, Skuld, NorthStandard, London P&I Club) cancelled war risk coverage effective March 5. Trump: $20B DFC reinsurance (Chubb lead). JPMorgan: $352B actual exposure across 329 tankers. London Joint War Committee extended high-risk area to include Oman waters.

alternative_routes.name: East-West Pipeline (Saudi Petroline)
alternative_routes.status: FULLY ACTIVATED. Yanbu exports surged 330%. But 70-75% of exports must transit Bab el-Mandeb. Yanbu briefly hit by Iranian missile Mar 20. Effective throughput ~2.2-2.47 mbd.

alternative_routes.name: IPSA Pipeline (Iraq-Saudi)
alternative_routes.status: Mothballed since 1990 — would need 2-3 months rehabilitation

alternative_routes.name: Habshan-Fujairah ADCOP (UAE)
alternative_routes.status: OPERATIONAL at ~71% (~1.06M bpd). Fujairah loading terminal under repeated attack (5+ times since Mar 3). Ruwais refinery shut. ADNOC crude loadings suspended intermittently.

alternative_routes.name: Iraq-Kurdistan (Kirkuk-Ceyhan)
alternative_routes.status: RESTARTED Mar 18-19. First flow in decade at 250K bpd initial. Hydrostatic testing on 100km section. Baghdad-KRG agreement reached. Potential 450K bpd.

impact.shadow_tanker_transit_note: Shadow fleet ~50% of all transits. ~3 transits/day mostly Chinese-flagged and Iranian-permitted vessels. Iran operating selective passage system for friendly nations.
impact.vessels_attacked_note: 29 commercial ships attacked (13 tankers) killing 11 seafarers. JMIC/UKMTO threat level: CRITICAL ('attack almost inevitable'). Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 — 'coordinated safe passage' announced but mine clearance required.
impact.kharg_island_strike_note: US struck 90+ military targets on Kharg Island Day 14-15. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared.
impact.south_pars_strike_note: Israeli Air Force struck South Pars Phases 3/4/5/6 and Asaluyeh hub with US coordination. First upstream energy strike. ~100M cm³/day gas offline. Two refineries halted. Qatar condemned.
impact.natanz_strike_note: Joint US-Israeli strike on Natanz nuclear enrichment facility March 21. IAEA confirmed no radiation increase. DG Grossi called for 'military restraint.' Russia condemned. Israel denied responsibility. Second Natanz strike in Operation Epic Fury.
impact.hormuz_aerial_campaign_note: US began aerial campaign to reopen Strait March 19 using A-10 Warthogs and Apache gunships against Iranian fast-attack boats, drones, and coastal positions.
impact.iran_selective_passage_note: Iran operating de facto selective blockade — vessel registration and vetting system. Friendly nations (China, India, Pakistan, Turkey, Japan under negotiation) allowed passage. Iran exporting ~1.5 mbd to China uninterrupted. At least one tanker paid $2M for Larak Island corridor transit. US allies blocked.
impact.us_escort_status: DAY 45 — BLOCKADE EFFECTIVE 14:00 GMT: Trump's US naval blockade of Iranian ports takes effect Apr 13 14:00 GMT. CENTCOM narrowed scope to Iranian ports only (not all Hormuz traffic) — significant divergence from Trump Truth Social announcement. IRGC declared any military vessel approaching the Strait a ceasefire violation to be met with 'harsh and decisive' force. Iran's armed forces called the blockade 'an illegal act amounting to piracy.' UK explicitly declined to participate; Starmer: 'not getting dragged into the war.' Australia not asked. CENTCOM: 'additional U.S. forces, including underwater drones, will join the clearance effort.' Historic Apr 11 precedent: USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) transited the Strait for mine-clearing. WSJ Apr 13: 60%+ of IRGC's naval fleet tasked with Hormuz patrol remains intact — contradicts White House 97% mine elimination claim. Bush CSG (CVN-77) at/approaching Strait of Gibraltar — ETA Red Sea/Arabian Sea Apr 14-18. Lincoln (CVN-72) operating dark (AIS off) in Arabian Sea with CSG-3 (CVW-9). Ford (CVN-78) in Eastern Mediterranean SW of Cyprus — deployment at 293 days, breaks post-Vietnam record (294 days, Lincoln 2019-20) ~Apr 14. USS Harry S. Truman (CVN-75) NOT deployed — at Norfolk for post-deployment maintenance. Tripoli ARG + 31st MEU operational in Arabian Sea. Boxer (LHD-4) in transit with 11th MEU — ETA late April (Apr 22-28). 82nd Airborne 1st BCT forward-deployed (~2,000 paratroopers). Atlantic Council (Apr 10): 63-71% AWACS fleet deployed, ~33% tanker aircraft, 29-43% THAAD committed. 16 MQ-9 Reapers lost since Epic Fury began. USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 6: 108 deployed of 291 battle force (78 USS, 30 USNS). Total ~30 warships in region. CENTCOM: 13,000+ targets struck, 80% Iran air defenses destroyed, every Iranian sub sunk, 97% naval mines eliminated. Two-week ceasefire clock expires ~Apr 21. 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29 — 16 days. Congress returned Apr 14.
impact.us_sanctions_lift_note: US Treasury temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil already loaded on ships (effective Mar 21 until Apr 19), expecting ~140M barrels to global supply. Iran dismissed, saying it has no surplus crude to sell.
impact.irgc_threat_march_18: IRGC issued 'urgent warning' following South Pars strike — named SAMREF Refinery (Saudi), Jubail Petrochemical Complex (Saudi), Al Hosn Gas Field (UAE), Mesaieed (Qatar), Ras Laffan (Qatar) as 'direct and legitimate targets in the coming hours.' Urged civilian/worker evacuation.
impact.ruwais_refinery_status: Shut — Abu Dhabi's largest refinery (922,000 bpd) offline after earlier drone strikes.
impact.day_22_events: Day 22 — Nowruz/Eid — no operational pause. 8,000+ US targets, 8,000+ sorties, 130 vessels destroyed. Natanz struck. 5,000-lb bunker-busters on Hormuz coastal sites. Aerial campaign continues (A-10s, Apaches). Diego Garcia attacked from 2,500mi. Dimona struck. 9 salvos at Israel. 22 countries condemn closure. South Korea joins coalition. US lifts sanctions on loaded Iranian oil. Brent ~$108. S&P 6,506 (6-month low). Goldman warns could exceed $147. JPMorgan: 'policy measures may have limited impact unless safe passage assured.'
impact.day_23_events: Day 23 — Trump 48-hour ultimatum: 'obliterate power plants' if Hormuz not reopened by Mar 23 7:44 PM ET. Iran responds: threatens destruction of all regional energy infrastructure. CENTCOM Adm. Cooper video: 8,000+ targets, 130 vessels, Iran 'on steady decline.' GBU-72 destroys underground coastal missile facility. IDF 'wave of extensive strikes' on Hezbollah. USS Tripoli arrival imminent. Iraq force majeure on oilfields. Brent $112.19 (Fri close). Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipeline restarted 250K bpd. Japan offered safe passage — declined. Houthis warn but still inactive.
impact.day_24_events: Day 24 — Trump postpones power plant strikes 5 days, claims 'productive conversations' with Iran via intermediaries (Oman, Turkey, Egypt, Pakistan). Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf is key Iranian interlocutor — no direct talks, per Iran FM. Brent crashes 13.6% to $96.91 intraday (largest single-day swing of war) then recovers to ~$104. S&P surges +1.7-2.2%. IDF strikes Tehran; Iran strikes Netanya. Gulf states intercept fresh barrages. Saudi expels Iranian military attaché. Ford arrives Souda Bay for repairs. IEA: 44+ energy assets damaged across 9 countries, 'worst since combined 1973+1979.' 3,000+ vessels stranded. 600+ in immediate Gulf. $2M IRGC passage corridor. Houthis still dormant — ~30 tankers near Yanbu within range. New deadline ~Mar 28.
impact.day_26_events: Day 26 — 82nd Airborne deployment confirmed. 15-point ceasefire plan rejected by Iran as 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable.' Iran outlines 5-7 counter-conditions: closure of US bases in Gulf, reparations, Hormuz transit-fee arrangement, end to Israeli strikes on Hezbollah, lifting all sanctions, preservation of missile program. Iran's Hormuz letter to UN/IMO: 'non-hostile vessels' may transit after coordination; no passage for US/Israeli vessels. Brent $96.68 (−3.5%). S&P ~6,596 (+0.6%). Tripoli ARG in CENTCOM AOR. Boxer weeks out. Kuwait airport drone strike. Saudi intercepts 32+ drones. AP-NORC: 59% say war 'gone too far.' Speaker Johnson: 'almost done.'
impact.day_25_events: Day 25 — Iran launches 7 waves of ballistic missiles at Israel, 4 impacts in Tel Aviv, 6 wounded. IAF strikes 50+ targets overnight in Iran (IDF cumulative 3,000+). CENTCOM: 9,000+ targets, 140+ vessels destroyed, 9,000+ combat flights. 12+ underwater mines confirmed (Maham 3/7 types). CBS News confirms mine types. Kuwait alarms 7 times. Saudi intercepts ~20 drones. IRGC attacks US 5th Fleet in Bahrain — 1 Emirati KIA (first UAE combat death). UAE intercepts Abu Dhabi missile. Oil rebounds 3-4% to ~$103 Brent / ~$91 WTI after Iran denies all negotiations. Tripoli ARG enters CENTCOM AOR ~Mar 25. Lebanon expels Iran ambassador (deadline Mar 29). Israel DM Katz: occupy S. Lebanon to Litani. IDF strikes Beirut command centers. Pakistan emerging as lead mediator. Gas $3.956. ~$29.3B cumulative. Bahrain UNSC Chapter VII draft; France alternative. UK emergency meeting on economic fallout.
impact.day_27_events: Day 27 — Israel kills IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri in Bandar Abbas precision strike — architect of Hormuz blockade eliminated. Senior IRGC naval leadership killed. Iran formally responds to 15-point plan with 5-point counterproposal including Hormuz sovereignty. Brent surges to $105.85 (+3.8-6.4%). Trump: 'get serious soon, before it is too late — NO TURNING BACK.' Confirms 4-6 week war. Reveals Iran allowed ~8 tankers through as gesture. IDF 'wave of extensive strikes' on Isfahan arms factories. Iranian missiles hit 4 Israeli locations. 2 killed Abu Dhabi from debris. Hezbollah rocket kills man in Nahariya. IDF soldier killed in Lebanon. Senate blocks war powers 53-47. Pakistan pushing in-person talks Mar 28. Gas $3.98 (+$1.00/month). Power plant deadline ~Mar 28. Iran fortifying Kharg Island. 82nd Airborne en route. ~$31.8B cumulative.
impact.day_28_events: Day 28 — Trump extends power plant deadline 10 days to Apr 6; G7 summit fractures — no communiqué; IDF overnight blitz Tehran/Kermanshah/Dezful; CENTCOM: 10,000+ targets, 130+ vessels, 8,000+ flights, 'largest navy elimination since WWII'; Iran submits 5-point counterproposal including Hormuz sovereignty; Pakistan pushes in-person Islamabad talks this weekend; Brent $108.01; VLCC rates $423,736/day all-time high; ~2,000 vessels stranded; 20,000 seafarers; insurance 5-10% of hull value; Iran mines (12+, Maham-3/7) + 1,500 drone boats; Philippines declares emergency
impact.day_29_events: Day 29 — BREAKING: Houthis launch first missile barrage at Israel — third active front opens; Bab al-Mandeb closure threatened — dual chokepoint scenario; ~30 tankers near Yanbu within Houthi range; Brent $112.57 (+4.22%); WTI intraday $100.04; Dow enters correction; VIX 31.05; Trump extends Hormuz deadline to Apr 6; VP Vance takes lead on negotiations; Iran rejects 15-point plan; 10 US troops injured Prince Sultan AB; Kuwait airport radar damaged; UK preparing coalition to reopen strait; ~$34.3B cumulative
impact.day_30_events: Day 30 — Islamabad quadrilateral summit convenes (Pakistan/Saudi/Turkey/Egypt); IRGC university ultimatum expires noon Mar 30; Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure 'among our options' — 12% of global trade at risk; Israel strikes Arak heavy water + Ardakan yellowcake; CENTCOM: 11,000+ targets, 66% missile/drone production destroyed; Iran agreed 20 Pakistani-flagged ships through Hormuz; 142 total transits Mar 1-25 vs normal 3,800+; VLCC rates peaked $519K/day; SPR 172M bbl exchange underway; 3,200+ 'No Kings' protests all 50 states; Kaine-Paul war powers fails 47-53; ~$35.5B cumulative
impact.day_31_events: Day 31 — Trump threatens to 'completely obliterate' power plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and desalination plants if no deal; Pentagon preparing 'weeks of ground operations' including Kharg Island raids; Brent $114.90-$116+ — +51% in March, steepest monthly surge on record; Dubai physical $126 — gap reflects acute physical supply stress; Iran agreed 20 Pakistani-flagged vessels through Strait (2/day); Iran 'tollbooth' charging ~$2M per transit in yuan; insurance 5%+ of vessel value; ~400 vessels stranded Gulf of Oman, ~20,000 seafarers; Islamabad summit: both sides 'expressed confidence in Pakistan'; Apr 6 deadline one week out; Iranian Navy Cmdr Irani threatens Lincoln directly; Spain closes airspace to US planes; $23B arms sales bypass Congress; ~$36.8B cumulative
impact.day_32_events: Day 32 — Iranian drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at Port of Dubai — risks major oil spill at critical commercial port; 2 Chinese container ships successfully transited Mar 30 (first cargo vessels since war began); Houthi deputy info minister: 'closing the Bab al-Mandeb Strait is among our options' — dual chokepoint scenario; Brent $112.78 settle, nearing $120 on Al Salmi attack; +~55% monthly — largest gain since inception 1988; Goldman: could exceed $147; SocGen: up to $150; IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history'; gas crossed $4.00 nationally; diesel $5.42; Jones Act waiver considered; WSJ: Trump may end war without securing Hormuz — 'not a core objective'; Apr 6 deadline approaching; 15-point US plan vs 5-point Iran counter — vast gap; ~$38.1B cumulative
impact.day_33_events: Day 33 — Trump claims Iran 'asked for CEASEFIRE' — markets surge (S&P +2.91%); Brent drops 5% to ~$105 on peace hopes; tanker Aqua 1 struck off Qatar by cruise missile (crew evacuated); Kuwait airport fuel depots ablaze from drones; IRGC cluster bomb hits Bnei Brak — children critically wounded; 3 missile waves at Israel; IRGC threatens 17 US corporate HQs; 200 dynamic strikes in 24 hrs; Hezbollah cmdr killed; Houthi missile intercepted; Apr 6 deadline 5 days; VLCC rates $423,736/day all-time high; ~400 vessels stranded Gulf of Oman; Mar 1-25: only 142 transits vs 2,652 in same 2025 period; Strait remains closed since Mar 4; Pakistan FM in Beijing — China as guarantor; Rubio: 'finish line coming'; Trump address 9 PM ET; gas $4.064 — record monthly jump; 15 KIA / 348 WIA; ~$39.3B cumulative
impact.day_34_events: Day 34 — Strait remains closed; 11 ships transited Mar 31 (Windward); Mar 1-25 transits fell 93% to 142 (USNI/Lloyd's); 71% of transiting ships Iran-linked; IRGC 'toll booth' at Larak Island charging up to $2M per crossing in yuan or crypto; war-risk insurance 5-10% of hull value; ~2,000 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers stranded; first LNG tanker (Omani-flagged Sohar LNG) attempting transit since war began (Bloomberg); UK-led 35-nation coalition convened to discuss reopening — US did not participate; Starmer: 'will not be easy'; Macron: military force 'unrealistic'; Iraq began exporting oil by tanker truck through Syria; Bush CSG ETA Apr 10-12; Apr 6 deadline 4 days away; Pakistan acknowledges 'obstacles'; China-Pakistan 5-point peace initiative; Brent $107.15 (+50.9% from pre-war); vessels attacked total 23
impact.day_35_events: Day 35 — Good Friday — markets closed; Strait remains closed ~6 transits/day; Iran agreed to allow Philippine-flagged vessels after bilateral talks; Iran-Oman drafting monitored transit protocol (Apr 2 headline triggered market reversal); UK 40-country summit on reopening — US declined; GCC calls on UNSC to authorize force; Bahrain draft resolution for 'all defensive means necessary'; India and Pakistan sent destroyers to escort tankers; Iran parliament formalizing transit fees; IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history'; Gulf production down ~10M bpd; desalination plant strikes creating humanitarian crisis in Kuwait/Qatar; Gulf consumer prices +40-120%; Houthis holding fire on shipping but threatening Bab al-Mandeb closure; ~30 Saudi tankers at Yanbu within Houthi range; Ford departs Split; 3 CSGs committed; Apr 6 deadline 3 days; Brent $109.03 (+53.6%)
impact.day_36_events: Day 36 — BREAKTHROUGH: French CMA CGM container vessel (Maltese-flagged) successfully transited Apr 3 — first major Western European ship since hostilities; Japanese oil tanker followed hugging Omani coast; Iran selectively permitting China/India-bound vessels with escort; Pakistan secured deal for Pakistani-flagged ships; ~3,200 ships (~4% global tonnage) idle; 500+ waiting outside; IEA chief Birol: 'largest supply disruption in history — 12M bpd lost, more than two 1970s crises combined'; IEA largest-ever reserve release: 400M barrels across 32 members; Bloomberg Economics: Houthi Red Sea disruption could push oil to $140 — Saudi rerouted 5M bpd through Red Sea; Houthis 4 attacks in 7 days on Israel, holding fire on shipping but threatening Bab al-Mandeb blockade of 'aggressor countries'; Pakistan ceasefire talks collapsed; F-15E shot down (first US combat aircraft loss since 2003); Apr 6 deadline 2 days; Trump threatens to bomb Iran 'back to Stone Ages'; Brent $109.03
impact.day_37_events: Day 37 — April 6 ultimatum less than 24 hours away — Trump: 'Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one'; Sen. Graham confirms Trump intends to follow through with 'overwhelming military force'; Iran rejects ultimatum — Gen. Aliabadi: 'the gates of hell will open for you'; Bloomberg 7-day rolling transit average at war high; two Omani supertankers (Dhalkut, Habrut) each carrying 2M bbl via novel Musandam coastal route; hull war risk premiums 1.5-3% ($10-14M per VLCC transit); Lloyd's: 88% of underwriters still willing to write — ships staying away due to safety not insurance; IRGC charging $1/barrel tolls in yuan/stablecoins; dual chokepoint threat: Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb would block ~30% of global container shipping, ~22% of global oil supply, ~$10B/day trade; IEA: 'April will be much worse than March' as pre-war cargoes exhausted; 5th Houthi missile at Israel intercepted; ceasefire 2% by Apr 7; 24% by Apr 30
impact.day_38_events: Day 38 — Trump sets Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline for Iran to reopen Strait or face strikes on power plants and bridges; 45-day ceasefire proposal from Egyptian-Pakistani-Turkish mediators rejected by Iran — 'incompatible with ultimatums and threats'; OPEC+ agreed Apr 5 to increase output 206K bpd for May — analysts dismiss as 'academic' (less than 2% of disrupted supply); Lloyd's List: 53 transits last week up from 36 — still 90%+ below normal; 400+ vessels anchored Gulf of Oman; QatarEnergy force majeure since Mar 4; 20,000+ seafarers stranded; Brent ~$111.25; JPMorgan warns $150+; some model $200/bbl; Houthis have not yet resumed Red Sea shipping attacks — holding as strategic card; Houthi leader warns Bab al-Mandeb could be closed if Gulf Arab states join war; Trump admitted arming Iranian Kurdish groups; markets reopen Monday after Easter
impact.day_40_events: Day 40 — CEASEFIRE RALLY THEN FRACTURE: Apr 8 ceasefire rally — Brent closes $94.75 (-14%), WTI $94.41 (-16%, biggest drop since Apr 2020); S&P 6,782.81 (+2.51%), Dow +2.85% best day since Apr 2025; VIX 21.04 (-18.39%); Apr 7: 11 vessels transited via IRGC-controlled Northern Corridor. THEN: Israel launches 'Operation Eternal Darkness' Apr 8 morning — 50 fighter jets, 160 munitions, 100+ targets across Lebanon in 10 min — 254 killed, 1,160+ wounded. IRGC re-closes Hormuz in response. Apr 8: only 5 bulk carriers outbound by noon — NO major oil tankers. White House (Leavitt) claims strait 'open' — Bloomberg, CNBC, Windward contradict. Netanyahu confirms ceasefire excludes Lebanon. European leaders + EU demand inclusion. ~800 tankers, 3,200 total vessels stuck in Gulf. CENTCOM Apr 8: 13,000+ targets, 80% Iran air defenses destroyed, every sub sunk, 97% mines eliminated.
impact.day_41_events: Day 41 — CEASEFIRE 'ON VERGE OF COLLAPSE' (Soufan Center): Brent rebounds to ~$97-98; WTI rebounds to ~$97-98; $12-18 Hormuz risk premium re-embedded; S&P reverses intraday from 6,782.81 close to ~6,752.64; VIX climbs toward 23-26; Hezbollah launches retaliatory missiles on northern Israel early Apr 9; Iran parliamentary speaker formally accuses US of ceasefire breach; Windward: 'supervised pause, not reopened'; White House confirms Vance + Witkoff + Kushner for Islamabad talks Apr 10-11 (highest-level US-Iran engagement since 1979); Bush CSG ETA Apr 10-12; Pope Leo XIV Apr 11 peace vigil at St. Peter's; Hegseth/Caine: US forces remain; Trump CNBC: military ready for 'next conquest'; UN Human Rights Chief: Lebanon strikes 'nothing short of horrific'; Soufan Center April 9 headline: 'Already Tenuous Ceasefire in Iran Hovers on the Verge of Collapse.'
impact.day_42_events: Day 42 — CEASEFIRE ~72 HOURS — VANCE DEPARTS FOR ISLAMABAD: ADNOC CEO Sultan Al Jaber: 'The Strait of Hormuz is not open. Access is being restricted, conditioned and controlled.' Hegseth contradicts: 'the strait is open.' Only ~15 ships transited in first 48 hours vs pre-war ~110/day. 600+ vessels stranded including 325 loaded tankers (Lloyd's List Intelligence) with ~20,000 seafarers. Iran demanding military approval for each vessel and planning tolls exceeding $1M/ship. Iran released map indicating possible mining with designated shipping lanes. US Navy decommissioned last 4 Avenger-class minesweepers from Bahrain Jan 2026; defense officials told WSJ Navy escorts 'aren't currently feasible' — Strait described as Iranian 'kill box.' VP Vance departed for Islamabad with Witkoff + Kushner — highest-level US-Iran engagement since 1979; formal talks Saturday Apr 12. Iran delegation: Ghalibaf + Araghchi. Iran's Parliament Speaker says 3/10 ceasefire points violated. Brent eases to ~$96; WTI ~$98. S&P 6,824.66 (Apr 9 close +0.62%). Bush CSG ETA today-Sunday — dual-carrier capability. March CPI released. Macron says ~15 countries mobilized for defensive mission to reopen Hormuz. Pope vigil tomorrow. 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29.
impact.day_43_events: Day 43 — ISLAMABAD TALKS BEGIN — 'TEHRAN TOLLBOOTH': First direct US-Iran face-to-face since 1979 at Serena Hotel — VP Vance, Witkoff, Kushner meet Ghalibaf + Araghchi. Hormuz reopening central to negotiations. IRGC established 'Tehran Tollbooth' — alternative channel north of Larak Island requiring prior permission, charging up to $2M/vessel (yuan or crypto), capping at 15 ships/day. Only ~10-16 ships transited in first 3 days post-ceasefire vs ~135/day pre-war. 500-700 vessels over 10,000 DWT stranded (Lloyd's List); 325 tankers; 230 oil-laden ready to sail (ADNOC). 20,000+ seafarers aboard. War risk insurance ~8x pre-war. All 12 P&I clubs withdrew coverage — 'de facto closure by insurance.' Physical dated Brent traded at $131.97/bbl — $35 premium over futures ($96.66) reflecting extreme physical scarcity. 13M bpd shut in (Energy Aspects). TD Securities: ~1 billion barrels lost by end of April. Iran's 10-point plan demands Hormuz transit fees ratified in binding UNSC resolution. US demands unconditional reopening. Disputed frozen assets: Iran claims US agreed to release — US flatly denies. CNN: China preparing new air defense delivery to Iran. Bush CSG approaching Gibraltar — ETA Red Sea Apr 14-18. Pope Leo XIV peace vigil noon ET. Congress returns Apr 14. 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29.
impact.day_44_events: Day 44 — TRUMP DECLARES US NAVAL BLOCKADE OF HORMUZ: Islamabad talks collapsed after 21 hours Apr 12 with no agreement. Vance press conference: 'We have not reached an agreement' — identifies nuclear enrichment as core sticking point, leaves 'final and best offer.' Five gaps: nuclear, Hormuz, Lebanon, sanctions/frozen assets, reparations. Araghchi and Witkoff 'nearly came to blows' during Hormuz discussions (Turkish media). Ghalibaf five-point X post blames US: 'the other side was ultimately unable to gain the trust of the Iranian delegation.' Trump immediately announces blockade via Truth Social: 'Effective immediately, the United States Navy, the Finest in the World, will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz.' Orders Navy to interdict every vessel that paid Iran's toll, destroy Iranian mines. Threatens: 'Any Iranian who fires at us, or at peaceful vessels, will be BLOWN TO HELL!' / 'At an appropriate moment, we are fully LOCKED AND LOADED, and our Military will finish up the little that is left of Iran!' Iran's deputy parliament speaker Nikzad: victory determined by 'the will of nations... not by rhetoric on social media.' KEY COMPLICATION: Iran reportedly lost track of mines it planted. ANOTHER: Chinese, Indian, and Pakistani ships transiting under Iranian deals could escalate into broader confrontation. Ceasefire on Iran front technically holds ~120 hours (expires ~Apr 22) but future uncertain. Historic Apr 11 precedent: USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) transited Strait for mine-clearing — first US warships since war began. Bush CSG at/approaching Gibraltar — ETA Red Sea Apr 14-18. Markets closed weekend — Monday reopening expected to reverse much of last week's ceasefire rally.
impact.day_45_events: Day 45 — BLOCKADE EFFECTIVE 14:00 GMT — IRGC WARNS OF 'HARSH AND DECISIVE' FORCE: Trump's US naval blockade takes effect April 13 14:00 GMT, approximately 24 hours after Islamabad talks collapsed. CRITICAL DIVERGENCE: Trump's Truth Social declaration threatened all Hormuz traffic, but CENTCOM's operational order narrowed scope — blockade applies only to ships entering or exiting Iranian ports and 'will not impede freedom of navigation for vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz to and from non-Iranian ports.' IRGC warned military vessels approaching the Strait 'will be considered a violation of the ceasefire and will be met with severe force.' Iran's armed forces called the blockade 'an illegal act amounting to piracy.' IRGC: 'Security in the Persian Gulf and the Sea of Oman is either for everyone or for NO ONE.' Ghalibaf taunts: 'Soon you'll be nostalgic for $4-$5 gas.' UK explicitly declines — Starmer: 'not getting dragged into the war.' Australia not asked. EU's von der Leyen: EU fossil fuel import costs +€22B in 44 days. CENTCOM announces 'additional U.S. forces, including underwater drones, will join the clearance effort.' WSJ Apr 13: 60%+ of IRGC's naval fleet tasked with Hormuz patrol remains intact — contradicts White House 97% mine elimination claim. Oil surged back above $100: Brent ~$103 (+7.3%), WTI $97-105 intraday. S&P ~6,775-6,781 (-0.5-0.6%); VIX 21.30 (+10.8%). Trump says ceasefire 'holding well,' 'their military is destroyed, their whole navy is under water' — zero new Iranian casualties since Apr 7 (ceasefire ~144 hours). Lavan Oil Refinery Apr 11 remains most recent alleged violation (unclear attribution). Next 48 hours: most dangerous since Apr 7 ceasefire. Ceasefire expires ~Apr 21. Ford deployment 293 days — breaks post-Vietnam record (294 days, Lincoln 2019-20) ~Apr 14. Bush CSG at/approaching Gibraltar.
